content:
You are a codebase exploration specialist focused exclusively on searching and analyzing existing code.
Your main goal is to explore the codebase based on a query, which are denoted by the <query> tag.

Your strengths:
- Rapidly finding files using glob patterns
- Searching code and text with powerful regex patterns
- Reading and analyzing file contents

Guidelines:
- For file searches: search broadly when you don't know where something lives. Use Read when you know the specific file path.
- For analysis: Start broad and narrow down. Use multiple search strategies if the first doesn't yield results.
- Be thorough: Check multiple locations, consider different naming conventions, look for related files.

NOTE: You are meant to be a fast agent that returns output as quickly as possible. In order to achieve this you must:
- Make efficient use of the tools that you have at your disposal: be smart about how you search for files and implementations
- Wherever possible you should try to spawn multiple parallel tool calls for grepping and reading files


## Required Output

End your response with an optional brief explanation of your findings (no more than 50 words), followed by a `<final_answer>` tag containing the relevant file paths and line ranges.

<example>
The core routing logic lives in two files.

<final_answer>
/absolute/path/to/file_1.py:10-15 (Optional Brief Reason: e.g., "Core logic to modify")
/absolute/path/to/file_2.js:102-123
</final_answer></example>

## Working Environment

OS Version: Darwin

Shell: /bin/zsh

Workspace Path:/Users/czeigler/Documents/Projects/agfDeploymentDocs

The directory listing of the workspace is:
```
.DS_Store
docs
README.md
.playwright-mcp
manifests
.git
```

Now, complete the user's search request efficiently and report your findings clearly.
Lead Nurture Agent SDR prospecting agent deployment metadata sandbox to prod constraint

```/Users/czeigler/Documents/Projects/agfDeploymentDocs/manifests/lead-nurture-agent-package.xml:1-90
1|<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
2|<!--
3|  LEAD NURTURE AGENT — DEPENDENCIES MANIFEST  (TEMPLATE — not deploy-ready)
4|  ============================================================================
5|  FOR LEAD NURTURE AGENT - DEPENDENCIES ONLY.
6|      This manifest excludes the Lead Nurture Agent itself.
7|      The AGENT is rebuilt from the "Lead Nurture Agent" template in the target org's
8|      setup flow and Agentforce Builder. See docs/12-lead-nurture-agent.md.
9|      Salesforce documents metadata deployment/retrieval as unsupported for the
10|      packaged Lead Nurture Agent, even when behavior varies by org.
11|
12|      NOTE: a CUSTOM Agent Script sales outreach agent (one with its own .agent
13|      source) uses the normal Agent Script path. Deploy it like a Service or
14|      Employee Agent using docs/10-service-agent.md or docs/11-employee-agent.md instead.
15|  ============================================================================
16|  Replace every placeholder and Object.Field__c with real API names, and remove
17|  any block you don't use before handoff.
18|
19|  Data 360 dependencies are separate. Use a Data Kit manifest from
20|  docs/20-data-360-data-kit.md; do not mix Data 360 metadata into this dependencies package.
21|-->
22|<Package xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata">
23|
24|  <!-- Sales data dependencies the agent reads (qualification / scoring / routing) -->
25|  <types>
26|    <members>Lead.Custom_Field__c</members>
27|    <members>Contact.Custom_Field__c</members>
28|    <name>CustomField</name>
29|  </types>
30|
31|  <!-- Custom objects, if you added nurture-tracking data -->
32|  <!--
33|  <types>
34|    <members>NURTURE_CUSTOM_OBJECT__c</members>
35|    <name>CustomObject</name>
36|  </types>
37|  -->
38|
39|  <!-- Customized email / response prompt templates (must be Published) -->
40|  <types>
41|    <members>PROMPT_TEMPLATE_API_NAME</members>
42|    <name>GenAiPromptTemplate</name>
43|  </types>
44|
45|  <!-- Custom actions ONLY if you added non-standard ones (the template ships its own) -->
46|  <!--
47|  <types>
48|    <members>APEX_CLASS_API_NAME</members>
49|    <members>APEX_CLASS_TEST_API_NAME</members>
50|    <name>ApexClass</name>
51|  </types>
52|  <types>
53|    <members>FLOW_API_NAME</members>
54|    <name>Flow</name>
55|  </types>
56|  -->
57|
58|  <!-- Custom outreach email template, if separate from Salesforce setup.
59|       Mailboxes, EAC connection, generated emails, cadence runtime state,
60|       and Builder activation state stay in target-org setup. -->
61|  <!--
62|  <types>
63|    <members>EMAIL_TEMPLATE_FOLDER/EMAIL_TEMPLATE_API_NAME</members>
64|    <name>EmailTemplate</name>
65|  </types>
66|  -->
67|
68|  <!-- Permission set: must include the current Lead Nurture Agent setup permission + data access -->
69|  <types>
70|    <members>LEAD_NURTURE_PERMISSION_SET_API_NAME</members>
71|    <name>PermissionSet</name>
72|  </types>
73|
74|  <!-- Callout config for enrichment / outbound systems, if used -->
75|  <!--
76|  <types>
77|    <members>NAMED_CREDENTIAL_API_NAME</members>
78|    <name>NamedCredential</name>
79|  </types>
80|  <types>
81|    <members>EXTERNAL_CREDENTIAL_API_NAME</members>
82|    <name>ExternalCredential</name>
83|  </types>
84|  -->
85|
86|  <!-- Data 360 metadata belongs in a separate DevOps Data Kit package.
87|       See docs/20-data-360-data-kit.md. -->
88|
89|  <version>66.0</version>
90|</Package>

```

```/Users/czeigler/Documents/Projects/agfDeploymentDocs/docs/12-lead-nurture-agent.md:1-202
1|# Deploy Lead Nurture Agent Dependencies
2|
3|Move custom dependencies for Lead Nurture Agent.
4|
5|**Use this guide for custom dependencies only.** Do not move Lead Nurture Agent itself, or Lead Nurture Agent changes, by change set, Metadata API, or Salesforce CLI. Create and configure the agent directly in the target org after deploying dependencies.
6|
7|## What can be deployed
8|
9|| Scope | Items |
10||---|---|
11|| Moves with metadata | Custom fields, custom objects, permission sets, Apex, tests, Flows, prompt template overrides, callout configuration for custom actions, and separate custom email templates |
12|| Configure in the target org | Lead Nurture Agent, agent user, mailbox, Einstein Activity Capture, sender behavior, cadence, data library, Builder preview, activation, generated emails, and runtime state |
13|
14|Legacy project actions can move separately with [Legacy Agent Actions](13-legacy-agent-actions.md). They can reduce manual rebuild work because the target agent can add them from the Asset Library.
15|
16|## Prepare the dependency package
17|
18|Copy `manifests/lead-nurture-agent-package.xml` to `manifest/package.xml`; replace XML-safe placeholders with real API names.
19|
20|Common dependencies:
21|
22|| Dependency | Metadata type |
23||---|---|
24|| Custom Lead, Contact, or Account fields | `CustomField` |
25|| Custom nurture tracking objects | `CustomObject` |
26|| Custom prompt template overrides | `GenAiPromptTemplate` |
27|| Custom invocable actions and tests | `ApexClass`, `Flow` |
28|| Custom email templates or custom copies, if separate from Salesforce setup | `EmailTemplate` |
29|| Callout configuration for custom enrichment or outbound actions | `NamedCredential`, `ExternalCredential` |
30|| Data access and setup permissions | `PermissionSet` |
31|| Data 360 dependencies | Separate Data Kit package |
32|| Custom legacy agent actions | Separate legacy agent actions package |
33|
34|Package rules:
35|
36|- Include only confirmed custom flows, prompts, Apex actions, and email templates.
37|- Do not assume generic Lead, email, or nurture-related flows belong to Lead Nurture Agent.
38|- Package only confirmed custom email templates or custom copies.
39|- Treat Salesforce-provided templates, including `Email Templates from Salesforce`, as Salesforce setup artifacts.
40|- Leave Salesforce-provided Lead Nurture Agent templates, generated emails, draft emails, sent-email history, mailbox connections, EAC auth, cadence runtime state, and Builder activation state out of the package.
41|- Review prompt templates for `{!$Input:...}`, `{!$Flow:...}`, `templateDataProviders`, and `SOBJECT://...`.
42|- Include required fields, features, provider flows/actions, permissions, and callout configuration, or update the prompt before handoff.
43|
44|**Stop if:** A prompt template validation fails with an invalid merge field, missing data provider, or missing output schema. Fix the target prerequisite, include the dependency, or remove that prompt from the package before deploy.
45|
46|## Retrieve and deploy dependencies
47|
48|Log in to the source sandbox and retrieve the dependency package:
49|
50|```bash
51|sf org login web --json --alias <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS> --instance-url https://test.salesforce.com
52|sf org display --json --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
53|sf project retrieve start --json --manifest manifest/package.xml --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
54|```
55|
56|Confirm the retrieve result is `Succeeded`.
57|
58|Review the package before deploy:
59|
60|- Every `package.xml` member has a matching file under `force-app/main/default`.
61|- The package contains only custom dependencies.
62|- The package does not include Lead Nurture Agent itself.
63|- The package does not contain mailbox connections, EAC auth, generated emails, sent-email history, credential secrets, OAuth tokens, connector auth, or runtime state.
64|
65|Log in to the target org and confirm the org. Use `https://login.salesforce.com` for production or `https://test.salesforce.com` for a sandbox.
66|
67|```bash
68|sf org login web --json --alias <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --instance-url https://login.salesforce.com
69|sf org display --json --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
70|```
71|
72|Production deploys must run Apex tests. Validate first:
73|
74|```bash
75|sf project deploy validate --json --manifest manifest/package.xml --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --test-level RunLocalTests --wait 30
76|```
77|
78|If validation succeeds, copy `result.id` and quick deploy:
79|
80|```bash
81|sf project deploy quick --json --job-id <JOB_ID_FROM_VALIDATE> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --wait 30
82|```
83|
84|For sandbox validation, run a dry run first. If your sandbox release policy requires tests, replace `NoTestRun` with `RunLocalTests`.
85|
86|```bash
87|sf project deploy start --json --dry-run --manifest manifest/package.xml --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --test-level NoTestRun --wait 30
88|```
89|
90|If the dry run succeeds:
91|
92|```bash
93|sf project deploy start --json --manifest manifest/package.xml --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --test-level NoTestRun --wait 30
94|```
95|
96|Continue only after the deploy result is `Succeeded`.
97|
98|## Data 360 Data Kit
99|
100|If Lead Nurture Agent or its custom dependencies use Data 360 data, complete [Deploy a Data 360 Data Kit](20-data-360-data-kit.md) before configuring and previewing Lead Nurture Agent in the target org.
101|
102|Keep the Data 360 package separate from the Lead Nurture Agent dependency package. Confirm the target Data 360 components are deployed, connector access is reauthorized, required data is refreshed, and the Lead Nurture Agent users have the required Data 360 access.
103|
104|## Optional legacy agent actions
105|
106|Use only for custom legacy agent actions.
107|
108|- Does not move or update Lead Nurture Agent itself.
109|- Uses the `GenAiPlugin` and `GenAiFunction` path for legacy Agent Builder and saved Builder assets.
110|- Also applies to other Agentforce projects that use Asset Library assets.
111|
112|1. Prepare Lead Nurture Agent in the target org.
113|2. Deploy the legacy agent actions with [Legacy Agent Actions](13-legacy-agent-actions.md).
114|3. Open the target agent draft in Agentforce Builder.
115|4. Select **Add Resource** > **Add from Asset Library**.
116|5. Add the legacy project action.
117|6. Preview generated email behavior and action behavior before activation.
118|
119|Adding a legacy action to the target agent is a Builder step. The package only makes the action available.
120|
121|## Configure Lead Nurture Agent
122|
123|Lead Nurture Agent email, agent user, Einstein Activity Capture, data library, cadence, and activation are target-org configuration, not package metadata.
124|
125|In the target org:
126|
127|1. Turn on Lead Nurture Agent and supporting features.
128|2. Assign manager and sales-user permissions.
129|3. Create or select the Lead Nurture Agent user.
130|4. Connect the agent user's email account.
131|5. Confirm Einstein Activity Capture uses user-level authentication for the agent user.
132|6. Confirm the connected email address matches the Email field on the Lead Nurture Agent user record and is not connected to another user.
133|7. Configure the Lead Nurture Agent data library.
134|8. Configure behavior, cadence, language, tone, assignment, meeting scheduling, opt-out handling, and activation in Builder.
135|
136|Sales users must connect their own email accounts to EAC if they need to see, edit, reschedule, or cancel agent emails.
137|
138|## Validate email setup
139|
140|Lead Nurture Agent email is target-org runtime configuration.
141|
142|Package can include:
143|
144|- Custom email templates
145|- Prompt overrides
146|- Fields
147|- Actions
148|- Permissions
149|
150|Package does not connect mailboxes or create Builder activation state.
151|
152|1. Use a test lead or an approved lead record.
153|2. Confirm the Lead Nurture Agent user has an active email account connection.
154|3. Confirm Einstein Activity Capture is active for the agent user and any sales users who manage agent emails.
155|4. Confirm sender address, cadence, send caps, meeting-booking source, opt-out handling, and assignment rules.
156|5. If Send as Seller is on, confirm record owners have Inbox and Einstein Activity Capture access.
157|6. Preview Lead Nurture Agent in Builder and confirm the generated email uses the expected prompt templates and source values.
158|7. Do not enable automatic sending until the previewed email behavior is approved.
159|
160|## Fill the implementation worksheet
161|
162|Capture before handoff:
163|
164|| Setting | Value |
165||---|---|
166|| Company or product context | |
167|| Tone and persona | |
168|| Email send caps and cadence | |
169|| Agent sender mailbox | |
170|| Agent user EAC status | |
171|| Sales-user EAC requirement | |
172|| Meeting booking source | |
173|| Lead assignment rules | |
174|| Opt-out process | |
175|| Project prompt template overrides | |
176|| Data library or knowledge source | |
177|| Data 360 dependency, if any | |
178|
179|Email sender, EAC auth, meeting links, cadence, opt-out behavior, and data-library choices are target-org values. Package only custom dependencies.
180|
181|## Checklist
182|
183|- [ ] Dependency package does not include Lead Nurture Agent itself.
184|- [ ] Custom fields, objects, prompt templates, actions, email templates, callout configuration, and permission sets are included when used.
185|- [ ] Legacy project actions are packaged separately when used.
186|- [ ] Data 360 Data Kit completed separately before target-org preview, if used.
187|- [ ] Agent user, email connection, EAC, data library, cadence, assignment, and activation are documented as target-org steps, not `package.xml` members.
188|- [ ] Worksheet completed from the source org.
189|- [ ] Dependency deploy succeeded.
190|- [ ] Lead Nurture Agent is enabled in the target org.
191|- [ ] Agent user email and EAC connection are active.
192|- [ ] Required sales users connected email to EAC.
193|- [ ] Builder preview completed with a test lead or approved lead.
194|- [ ] Generated emails use the expected prompt templates and source values.
195|- [ ] Opt-out, assignment, and meeting-booking behavior confirmed.
196|
197|## Sources
198|
199|- Salesforce Help: Lead Nurture Agent considerations: https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sales.sales_agent_sdr_considerations.htm&type=5
200|- Agentforce metadata deployment and retrieval limitations knowledge article: https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=005228853&type=1
201|- Salesforce Help: Set up Lead Nurture Agent: https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sales.einstein_sdr_setup.htm&type=5
202|- Retrieve and deploy Agentforce metadata: https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/ai/agentforce/guide/agent-dx-deploy-metadata.html

```

```/Users/czeigler/Documents/Projects/agfDeploymentDocs/docs/deployment-workflow.md:1-285
1|# Deploy a Package
2|
3|Use this page as a general Salesforce CLI reference for package folder setup, retrieve, validate, deploy, publish, activate, rollback, and go-live proof. The agent guides include these steps in context for each primary path.
4|
5|## Use This Page For
6|
7|| Need | Where to go |
8||---|---|
9|| Choose what kind of package you are moving | [Overview](index.md) |
10|| Build package-specific `package.xml` members | The selected package guide |
11|| Look up shared retrieve, validate, and deploy commands | This page |
12|| Look up Service or Employee Agent publish and activate commands | This page |
13|| Data Kit component deployment, web messaging channel, Lead Nurture Agent email, or Legacy Agent Actions | The selected package guide |
14|
15|## 1. Create the package folder
16|
17|Create or open one Salesforce DX project folder per deployment package:
18|
19|```text
20|deploy-package/
21|+-- sfdx-project.json
22|+-- manifest/
23||   +-- package.xml
24|+-- force-app/main/default/
25|```
26|
27|Use this minimal `sfdx-project.json`:
28|
29|```json
30|{
31|  "packageDirectories": [
32|    {
33|      "path": "force-app",
34|      "default": true
35|    }
36|  ],
37|  "name": "agentforce-deploy-package",
38|  "sourceApiVersion": "66.0"
39|}
40|```
41|
42|Save the package-specific manifest as `manifest/package.xml`, or use the path you will pass to `--manifest`.
43|
44|Before retrieving or deploying:
45|
46|- Replace all manifest placeholders with exact API names.
47|- Remove unused template blocks.
48|- Keep only one package type in the folder.
49|- Keep Data 360 metadata out of agent packages.
50|- Keep Lead Nurture Agent itself out of dependency packages.
51|- Remove source-org usernames, website domains, generated snippets, credential secrets, OAuth tokens, connector auth, and runtime state.
52|
53|## 2. Retrieve source files when needed
54|
55|Skip this section if the package folder already contains the reviewed source files.
56|
57|Log in to the source sandbox and confirm the org:
58|
59|```bash
60|sf org login web --json --alias <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS> --instance-url https://test.salesforce.com
61|sf org display --json --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
62|```
63|
64|Use these checks when you need exact API names from the source sandbox:
65|
66|```bash
67|sf org list metadata --json --metadata-type AiAuthoringBundle --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
68|sf org list metadata --json --metadata-type GenAiPlugin --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
69|sf org list metadata --json --metadata-type GenAiFunction --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
70|sf org list metadata --json --metadata-type GenAiPromptTemplate --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
71|sf org list metadata --json --metadata-type LightningTypeBundle --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
72|```
73|
74|For Data Kits, use the source Data Kit generated manifest. Do not hand-build the Data Kit manifest from this page.
75|
76|Retrieve from the package folder:
77|
78|```bash
79|sf project retrieve start --json --manifest <PACKAGE_XML_PATH> --target-org <SOURCE_ORG_ALIAS>
80|```
81|
82|Confirm the retrieve result is `Succeeded`.
83|
84|Use a two-pass retrieve for Service and Employee Agent source packages when backing dependencies are unknown:
85|
86|1. Retrieve the agent source with the `AiAuthoringBundle` member first.
87|2. Open `force-app/main/default/aiAuthoringBundles/<AGENT_API_NAME>/<AGENT_API_NAME>.agent`.
88|3. Search for `apex://` and add each invocable Apex class and test class to `package.xml`.
89|4. Search for `flow://` and add each Flow to `package.xml`.
90|5. Add referenced prompt templates, objects, fields, permission sets, credentials, and Custom Lightning Types only when the Service or Employee Agent source uses them.
91|6. Run retrieve again with the completed manifest.
92|
93|Review the package before deploy:
94|
95|- Every `package.xml` member has a matching file under `force-app/main/default`.
96|- The package contains only the package type being moved.
97|- The package does not contain source-org usernames, website domains, generated Web Chat snippets, credential secrets, OAuth tokens, connector auth, or runtime state.
98|- The package guide lists the target-org steps that remain after deploy.
99|
100|## 3. Confirm the target org
101|
102|Install and command checks:
103|
104|```bash
105|sf --version
106|sf agent --help
107|```
108|
109|For Data 360 packages:
110|
111|```bash
112|sf data360 --help
113|```
114|
115|**Stop if:** A required command group is unavailable and the machine cannot install or load Salesforce CLI plugins.
116|
117|Log in to the target org and confirm the org details.
118|
119|Production:
120|
121|```bash
122|sf org login web --json --alias <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --instance-url https://login.salesforce.com
123|sf org display --json --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
124|```
125|
126|Sandbox:
127|
128|```bash
129|sf org login web --json --alias <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --instance-url https://test.salesforce.com
130|sf org display --json --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
131|```
132|
133|For Service or Employee Agent packages, confirm Agentforce metadata is available:
134|
135|```bash
136|sf org list metadata --json --metadata-type AiAuthoringBundle --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
137|```
138|
139|**Stop if:** The command returns `INVALID_TYPE` or `Not available for deploy for this organization`. Enable and provision Agentforce for the target org before continuing.
140|
141|If the agent uses Data 360, complete [Deploy a Data 360 Data Kit](20-data-360-data-kit.md) before publishing the agent.
142|
143|```bash
144|sf api request rest "/services/data/v67.0/ssot/data-kits" --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --stream-to-file data-kits-check.json
145|```
146|
147|Open `data-kits-check.json`. Stop on `FUNCTIONALITY_NOT_ENABLED`, `CdpDataKit`, or any `errorCode`.
148|
149|## 4. Validate and deploy
150|
151|Production deploys must run Apex tests. Validate first:
152|
153|```bash
154|sf project deploy validate --json --manifest <PACKAGE_XML_PATH> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --test-level RunLocalTests --wait 30
155|```
156|
157|If validation succeeds, copy `result.id` and quick deploy:
158|
159|```bash
160|sf project deploy quick --json --job-id <JOB_ID_FROM_VALIDATE> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --wait 30
161|```
162|
163|**Stop if:** You do not have the job ID from a successful `sf project deploy validate` command. Do not quick deploy a sandbox dry-run job ID.
164|
165|Alternative: quick deploy the most recent successful validation:
166|
167|```bash
168|sf project deploy quick --json --use-most-recent --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --wait 30
169|```
170|
171|**Stop if:** The validation output includes Apex but reports zero tests run. Send the JSON output to the deployment owner before deploying.
172|
173|For sandbox validation, run a dry run first. If your sandbox release policy requires tests, replace `NoTestRun` with `RunLocalTests`.
174|
175|```bash
176|sf project deploy start --json --dry-run --manifest <PACKAGE_XML_PATH> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --test-level NoTestRun --wait 30
177|```
178|
179|If the dry run succeeds:
180|
181|```bash
182|sf project deploy start --json --manifest <PACKAGE_XML_PATH> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --test-level NoTestRun --wait 30
183|```
184|
185|Check deploy status with `result.id`:
186|
187|```bash
188|sf project deploy report --json --job-id <JOB_ID> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --wait 30
189|```
190|
191|If you no longer have the job ID:
192|
193|```bash
194|sf project deploy report --json --use-most-recent --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS> --wait 30
195|```
196|
197|Continue only after the deploy result is `Succeeded`.
198|
199|## 5. Publish Service and Employee Agents
200|
201|Use this section only for Service and Employee Agents. `sf project deploy` moves editable `AiAuthoringBundle` source. Publish creates a runnable version. Activate makes it available.
202|
203|Complete the target-org steps from the agent guide before publishing.
204|
205|Validate the deployed bundle:
206|
207|```bash
208|sf agent validate authoring-bundle --json --api-name <AGENT_API_NAME> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
209|```
210|
211|Start live preview before publishing:
212|
213|```bash
214|sf agent preview start --json --authoring-bundle <AGENT_API_NAME> --use-live-actions --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
215|```
216|
217|Use live preview for deployment readiness. Simulated preview can use mock data or relaxed checks; it does not prove target-org permissions, records, flows, prompts, callouts, or Data 360 access.
218|
219|Send a representative message with the returned `result.sessionId`:
220|
221|```bash
222|sf agent preview send --json --authoring-bundle <AGENT_API_NAME> --session-id <SESSION_ID> --utterance "Test the main happy path" --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
223|```
224|
225|End the preview:
226|
227|```bash
228|sf agent preview end --json --authoring-bundle <AGENT_API_NAME> --session-id <SESSION_ID> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
229|```
230|
231|Publish:
232|
233|```bash
234|sf agent publish authoring-bundle --json --api-name <AGENT_API_NAME> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
235|```
236|
237|Activate:
238|
239|```bash
240|sf agent activate --json --api-name <AGENT_API_NAME> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
241|```
242|
243|With `--json` and no `--version`, the CLI activates the latest published version automatically.
244|
245|For audited rollouts, activate an explicit version:
246|
247|```bash
248|sf agent activate --json --api-name <AGENT_API_NAME> --version <VERSION_NUMBER> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
249|```
250|
251|Activating a new version sends new sessions to that version. Existing sessions can continue on the version that started them.
252|
253|Smoke test the active agent:
254|
255|```bash
256|sf agent preview start --json --api-name <AGENT_API_NAME> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
257|sf agent preview send --json --api-name <AGENT_API_NAME> --session-id <SESSION_ID> --utterance "Test the main happy path" --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
258|sf agent preview end --json --api-name <AGENT_API_NAME> --session-id <SESSION_ID> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
259|```
260|
261|**Stop if:** Live preview fails, returns no expected data, or reports missing agent-user permissions. Fix target-org access before publishing.
262|
263|For clean-target Employee Agent deployments, deploy or update the permission set that contains `agentAccesses` only after the agent is published and active.
264|
265|To roll back a bad version, reactivate the prior known-good version:
266|
267|```bash
268|sf agent activate --json --api-name <AGENT_API_NAME> --version <PRIOR_VERSION_NUMBER> --target-org <TARGET_ORG_ALIAS>
269|```
270|
271|Capture the current and prior agent version numbers, session IDs, failing utterances, and deployment job ID for support. Deactivate the agent only when no version should be available to users.
272|
273|## 6. Capture go-live proof
274|
275|Save these values or screenshots with the deployment handoff:
276|
277|- Deployment job ID or Deployment Status screenshot.
278|- Apex test result summary when Apex was included.
279|- Active Service or Employee Agent version when an agent was published.
280|- Non-admin Employee Agent smoke test if employees will use the agent.
281|- Data Kit component deployment and refreshed target data if the agent uses Data 360.
282|- Approved Lead Nurture Agent email preview and mailbox/EAC setup if the deployment includes Lead Nurture Agent.
283|- Web messaging conversation test and `MessagingSession` ID if the agent is deployed to a web messaging channel.
284|
285|**Target-org evidence:** Screenshots and IDs prove readiness for one target org only. Do not reuse them for another org.

| Quick deploy cannot find the job | Quick deploy promotes a successful `sf project deploy validate` job, not a sandbox `--dry-run` job. |

The Lead Nurture Agent itself cannot be deployed via metadata; only its dependencies can. The agent and its core configuration must be set up directly in the target production environment. Production deployments also require Apex tests.
